name: ydisctools method-template overlay
description: Entries here are merged OVER the vendored siera catalog (method-library.json) by ydisctools::build_ars(); an entry with the same id replaces the vendored one. categorical_summary replaces siera's legacy distinct+dummy pattern with the modern cards denominator pattern (by = outer groupings, variables = the category variable), so generated ARDs carry the real variable name and levels (variable = AGEGR1, variable_level = '<65') instead of variable = 'dummy'. categorical_summary_flat is the dedicated single-branch template for a flat analysis (no category variable, e.g. 'subjects with at least one TEAE'): it tabulates a constant '.flag_' with the group as `by=`, so the percentage denominator is that group's big N. build_ars() picks the flat template automatically (compact parameters keep saying 'categorical_summary'); the split keeps every generated analysis free of dead branches, so the programmes stay readable and human-maintainable. Statistics are identical; verified against the dummy pattern on distinct-subject counts.

methods.name: Summary of a categorical variable (n and %)
methods.label: Categorical n (%)
methods.description: Distinct-subject n and percentage per category and group, with a referenced denominator analysis. Modern cards pattern: the category variable is passed as `variables=` (so it lands in the ARD's variable / variable_level columns) and the outer grouping(s) as `by=` (`strata=` when the innermost grouping is data-driven). Flat analyses (no category variable) are generated from the dedicated categorical_summary_flat template instead. ydisctools overlay entry; replaces the siera catalog's distinct+dummy template.
methods.verified_against: identical n/p to siera's Mth01_CatVar_Summ_ByGrp dummy pattern on distinct-subject counts

parameters.name: distinctlisthere
parameters.label: distinct list
parameters.description: Grouping variables + analysis variable, comma separated

parameters.name: denom_anagroupvarshere
parameters.label: denom grp var
parameters.description: Grouping variable(s) of the denominator analysis

parameters.name: byvarshere
parameters.label: by/variables args
parameters.description: by = outer grouping(s), variables = category variable

parameters.name: stratavarshere
parameters.label: strata/variables args
parameters.description: strata = outer grouping(s), variables = category variable

methods.templateCode:
denom_dataset = df2_denomanaidhere |>
  dplyr::select(denom_anagroupvarshere)

in_data = df2_analysisidhere |>
    dplyr::distinct(distinctlisthere)

# strata= when the innermost grouping is data-driven, by= when pre-defined
dataDriven = isdatadrivenhere
if(dataDriven == TRUE){
df3_analysisidhere <-
  cards::ard_tabulate(
    data = in_data
    stratavarshere,
    denominator = denom_dataset
  ) } else {
df3_analysisidhere <-
 cards::ard_tabulate(
    data = in_data
    byvarshere,
    denominator = denom_dataset
  ) }
df3_analysisidhere <- df3_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::filter(stat_name %in% c('n', 'p')) |>
  dplyr::mutate(operationid = dplyr::case_when(stat_name == 'n' ~ 'opid1here',
                                               stat_name == 'p' ~ 'opid2here'))

methods.name: Subject count per group (n and %)
methods.label: Flat n (%)
methods.description: Distinct-subject n and percentage per group for a flat analysis (no category variable, e.g. 'subjects with at least one TEAE'), with a referenced denominator analysis. Tabulates a constant '.flag_' with the group as `by=`, so the percentage denominator is that group's big N. Selected automatically by build_ars() when a categorical_summary analysis has a single grouping and variable = USUBJID; the compact parameter format keeps the categorical_summary key. ydisctools overlay entry.
methods.verified_against: per-group n/N against independently computed distinct-subject counts

parameters.name: distinctlisthere
parameters.label: distinct list
parameters.description: Grouping variable + analysis variable, comma separated

parameters.name: denom_anagroupvarshere
parameters.label: denom grp var
parameters.description: Grouping variable of the denominator analysis

methods.templateCode:
denom_dataset = df2_denomanaidhere |>
  dplyr::select(denom_anagroupvarshere)

# n (%) of subjects per group: tabulate a constant flag with the group as
# `by`, so the percentage denominator is that group's big N
in_data = df2_analysisidhere |>
    dplyr::distinct(distinctlisthere) |>
    dplyr::mutate(.flag_ = 'Y')

df3_analysisidhere <-
  cards::ard_tabulate(
    data = in_data
    , by = 'denom_anagroupvarshere', variables = '.flag_',
    denominator = denom_dataset
  )
df3_analysisidhere <- df3_analysisidhere |>
  dplyr::filter(stat_name %in% c('n', 'p')) |>
  dplyr::mutate(operationid = dplyr::case_when(stat_name == 'n' ~ 'opid1here',
                                               stat_name == 'p' ~ 'opid2here'))
